markdown.markdown:
Practical statistical guidance for CQC analysts. Methods, worked examples, and decision criteria, with complete R and Python code throughout.

This book teaches **judgment, not rules**: every section explains the reasoning behind analytical choices so you can adapt to your specific context.

---

::: {.callout-tip icon=false}
## Start Here

- **First time here?** → [How to Use This Book](chapters/01-getting-started.qmd) for orientation and reading strategies
- **Know your question?** → [Quick Reference](quick-reference.qmd) maps analytical problems directly to methods
- **Need QA guidance?** → [QA Principles](chapters/09-qa-principles.qmd) for the quality framework underpinning all analysis
:::

---

::: {.nav-cards}
::: {.nav-card .nav-card-foundations}
**Part I: Foundations**

Data types, descriptive statistics, variation, distributions, unusual observations, missing data concepts, statistical inference, and QA principles.

[View Foundations →](chapters/02-data-types.qmd)
:::

::: {.nav-card .nav-card-methods}
**Part II: Core Methods**

Z-scoring, SPC, t-tests, correlation, outlier detection, and missing data handling — each with step-by-step guides and dual R/Python code.

[View Methods →](chapters/10-z-scoring.qmd)
:::

::: {.nav-card .nav-card-examples}
**Part III: Worked Examples**

Complete analytical workflows using realistic CQC data, from data preparation through to stakeholder reporting.

[View Examples →](examples/README.qmd)
:::
:::

---

## How to Use This Guidance

There is no single right way to read this book. How you use it depends on what you need right now:

- **Building confidence?** Work through [Part I: Foundations](chapters/02-data-types.qmd) sequentially, then move to Core Methods and Worked Examples in order. See [How to Use This Book](chapters/01-getting-started.qmd) for a full reading guide.
- **Under pressure?** Jump straight to the method you need via the [Quick Reference](quick-reference.qmd), or find a similar scenario in Part III: Worked Examples.
- **Checking your approach?** Go to the relevant method chapter for assumptions, pitfalls, and interpretation guidance. Use the QA templates to document your decisions.

Every chapter uses a three-layer approach: **main text** for essentials, **Rethinking boxes** for context and judgment, and **Overthinking boxes** for edge cases and advanced detail. See [How to Use This Book](chapters/01-getting-started.qmd) for more on this structure.

---

::: {.callout-note icon=false}
## About This Version

**Version 1.0** (2026) covers 8 foundation chapters, 6 core methods, and 10 worked examples with dual R/Python code. For feedback or suggestions, contact the [Practice Lead for Quantitative Analytics and Statistics](mailto:federico.andreis@cqc.org.uk).

**Saving a page as PDF**: Use your browser's Print function (Ctrl+P / Cmd+P) and choose "Save as PDF". For best results, set margins to "Minimum" and enable "Background graphics".
:::
